frozen_components: original tokenizer and embeddings (used as reference during hypernetwork inference)
architecture.summary: A hypernetwork takes a target tokenizer as input (represented via token decompositions using an original tokenizer's vocabulary) and predicts embedding parameters for the target tokenizer's vocabulary. The hypernetwork is a Transformer-based model that learns to compose sequences of original embeddings into new embeddings suitable for any target tokenizer. During inference, predicted embeddings replace the original embeddings in the language model without modifying the LM's internal parameters.
trained_components: byte-level vocabulary extensions for token decomposition

claims.metric: accuracy drop and sequence length reduction
claims.statement: Zero-shot transfer to language-specific tokenizers with XLM-R preserves accuracy to 1% on average on XNLI, losing 3% in the worst case and improving by 1% in the best case, while reducing sequence length by 14% on average
Zero-shot transfer of Mistral-7B to GPT2 tokenizer on natural language benchmarks (PiQA, HellaSwag, ARC, BoolQ, MMLU) achieves 73.0% average accuracy compared to 76.9% for the original model

claims.dataset: HumanEvalPack (code: JavaScript, Go, Python, C++, Java)
claims.statement: Zero-shot transfer of Mistral-7B to StarCoder tokenizer achieves 23.2% average pass@1 on HumanEvalPack (code synthesis) compared to 28.1% for the original model; FOCUS baseline achieves 13.3%

claims.metric: benchmark accuracy average after continued training
claims.statement: Continued training for 800M tokens closes the gap between zero-shot hypernetwork transfer and original model performance on Mistral-7B, achieving 74.9% average accuracy on natural language benchmarks

Hypernetwork trained for base Mistral-7B can be applied to fine-tuned Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.1 without retraining, achieving 6.56 score on MT-Bench (single-model rating, 1-10 scale) compared to 7.33 for the original fine-tuned model

baseline.name: original XLM-R model on seen languages

claims.dataset: AmericasNLI (Aymara, Guarani) and FarsTail, SICK-NL (unseen tokenizers)
claims.statement: On completely unseen languages (Aymara, Guarani) not seen during hypernetwork training, XLM-R zero-shot transfer on AmericasNLI preserves accuracy to within 0-3% of original model while reducing sequence length by 36-39%

claims.dataset: PiQA, HellaSwag, ARC, BoolQ, MMLU and HumanEvalPack
claims.statement: Prior heuristic methods (FVT, FOCUS, OFA) for zero-shot tokenizer transfer perform significantly worse than the hypernetwork, with FOCUS achieving only 55.6% average accuracy on Mistral-7B natural language tasks (vs. 73.0% for the hypernetwork) and 13.3% pass@1 on code (vs. 23.2%)

Hypernetwork amortizes over the tokenization function and learns robust embeddings through diverse tokenizer sampling; predicted embeddings are robust to different tokenization functions applied to the same vocabulary, unlike original model embeddings which degrade significantly under alternative tokenization functions
